1→# Safari Circuits - Claude Code Team Standards Setup (v5.0)
     2→#
     3→# One-time bootstrap that creates symlinks to org-docs. After this, users just run `claude`.
     4→# Enterprise updates auto-sync via hook that pulls org-docs on session start.
     5→#
     6→# Usage:
     7→#   IT User:       .\setup-claude-team-standards.ps1 -Username dkmcintyre
     8→#   Business User: .\setup-claude-team-standards.ps1 -Username jsmith -UserType Business
     9→#   Force rebuild: .\setup-claude-team-standards.ps1 -Username dkmcintyre -Force
    10→#   Update MCPs:   .\setup-claude-team-standards.ps1 -SyncMcp
    11→#
    12→# Profiles:
    13→#   IT       - Full workflow: DevOps, GitHub, all commands/skills, user folder in repo
    14→#   Business - Document skills, MS-365 MCPs, no DevOps/GitHub write access
    15→#
    16→# After setup, users just run `claude` - no more manual steps!
    17→
    18→param(
    19→    [Parameter(Mandatory=$false)]
    20→    [string]$Username,            # GitHub username (creates user folder)
    21→
    22→    [ValidateSet("IT", "Business")]
    23→    [string]$UserType = "IT",      # IT (full) or Business (limited)
    24→
    25→    [switch]$Force,               # Overwrite existing symlinks/files
    26→
    27→    [switch]$SyncMcp              # Just regenerate ~/.mcp.json from template
    28→)
    29→
    30→$ErrorActionPreference = "Stop"
    31→
    32→# ── Quick MCP Sync Mode ───────────────────────────────────────────────
    33→
    34→if ($SyncMcp) {
    35→    # Auto-detect org-docs location from script path
    36→    $scriptDir = Split-Path -Parent $MyInvocation.MyCommand.Path
    37→    $orgDocsDir = Split-Path -Parent $scriptDir
    38→    $mcpTemplatePath = Join-Path $orgDocsDir ".claude\templates\mcp-config.json"
    39→    $mcpDestPath = Join-Path $env:USERPROFILE ".mcp.json"
    40→
    41→    # Pull latest first
    42→    if (Test-Path $orgDocsDir) {
    43→        Push-Location $orgDocsDir
    44→        git pull 2>&1 | Out-Null
    45→        Pop-Location
    46→    }
    47→
    48→    if (Test-Path $mcpTemplatePath) {
    49→        $mcpContent = Get-Content $mcpTemplatePath -Raw
    50→        $userHomeEscaped = $env:USERPROFILE -replace '\\', '\\'
    51→        $mcpContent = $mcpContent -replace '\{\{USER_HOME\}\}', $userHomeEscaped
    52→        $mcpContent | Set-Content $mcpDestPath -Force
    53→
    54→        $mcpJson = $mcpContent | ConvertFrom-Json
    55→        $mcpCount = ($mcpJson.mcpServers.PSObject.Properties | Measure-Object).Count
    56→        Write-Host "[sync] Updated ~/.mcp.json ($mcpCount MCP servers)" -ForegroundColor Green
    57→        Write-Host "       Restart your terminal for changes to take effect." -ForegroundColor Yellow
    58→    } else {
    59→        Write-Host "[error] MCP template not found. Run full bootstrap first." -ForegroundColor Red
    60→    }
    61→    exit 0
    62→}
    63→
    64→# Require Username for full bootstrap
    65→if (-not $Username) {
    66→    Write-Host "[error] -Username is required for full bootstrap." -ForegroundColor Red
    67→    Write-Host "        Use -SyncMcp to just update MCP config." -ForegroundColor Yellow
    68→    exit 1
    69→}
    70→
    71→# ── Paths ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    72→
    73→$claudeDir       = Join-Path $env:USERPROFILE ".claude"
    74→$orgDocsDir      = Join-Path $env:USERPROFILE "organizational-docs"
    75→$sourceClaudeDir = Join-Path $orgDocsDir ".claude"
    76→$enterpriseDir   = Join-Path $sourceClaudeDir "enterprise"
    77→$usersDir        = Join-Path $sourceClaudeDir "users"
    78→$userDir         = Join-Path $usersDir $Username
    79→$sourceTemplates = Join-Path $sourceClaudeDir "templates"
    80→
    81→# ── Helper Functions ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
    82→
    83→function Write-Log {
    84→    param([string]$Message, [string]$Color = "White")
    85→    Write-Host $Message -ForegroundColor $Color
    86→}
    87→
    88→function New-Symlink {
    89→    param(
    90→        [string]$Link,
    91→        [string]$Target,
    92→        [string]$Type = "SymbolicLink"  # SymbolicLink for files, Junction for directories
    93→    )
    94→
    95→    if (Test-Path $Link) {
    96→        if ($Force) {
    97→            Remove-Item $Link -Force -Recurse -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    98→        } else {
    99→            Write-Log "    Exists (use -Force to overwrite): $Link" "Yellow"
   100→            return $false
   101→        }
   102→    }
   103→
   104→    $parent = Split-Path $Link -Parent
   105→    if (-not (Test-Path $parent)) {
   106→        New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $parent -Force | Out-Null
   107→    }
   108→
   109→    try {
   110→        New-Item -ItemType $Type -Path $Link -Target $Target -Force | Out-Null
   111→        return $true
   112→    } catch {
   113→        Write-Log "    ERROR creating symlink: $_" "Red"
   114→        return $false
   115→    }
   116→}
   117→
   118→# ── Banner ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   119→
   120→Write-Log ""
   121→Write-Log ("=" * 64) "Cyan"
   122→Write-Log "  Safari Circuits - Claude Code Bootstrap v5.0" "Cyan"
   123→Write-Log "  Profile: $UserType | User: $Username" "Yellow"
   124→Write-Log ("=" * 64) "Cyan"
   125→Write-Log ""
   126→
   127→# ── Check for Admin ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   128→
   129→$isAdmin = ([Security.Principal.WindowsPrincipal] [Security.Principal.WindowsIdentity]::GetCurrent()).IsInRole([Security.Principal.WindowsBuiltInRole]::Administrator)
   130→if (-not $isAdmin) {
   131→    Write-Log "[!] WARNING: Not running as Administrator" "Yellow"
   132→    Write-Log "    Symlinks may fail. Re-run as Administrator if you get errors." "Yellow"
   133→    Write-Log ""
   134→}
   135→
   136→# ── Step 1: Clone/update org-docs ──────────────────────────────────────
   137→
   138→Write-Log "[1/6] Setting up organizational-docs repository..." "Yellow"
   139→
   140→if (-not (Test-Path $orgDocsDir)) {
   141→    Write-Log "  Cloning from GitHub..." "White"
   142→    Push-Location $env:USERPROFILE
   143→    try {
   144→        git clone "https://github.com/SafariCircuitsLLC/organizational-docs.git" 2>&1 | Out-Null
   145→        Write-Log "  Cloned successfully." "Green"
   146→    } catch {
   147→        Write-Host "[ERROR] Failed to clone. Ensure Git is installed and you have repo access." -ForegroundColor Red
   148→        Pop-Location
   149→        exit 1
   150→    }
   151→    Pop-Location
   152→} else {
   153→    Write-Log "  Found: $orgDocsDir" "Green"
   154→    Write-Log "  Pulling latest..." "White"
   155→    Push-Location $orgDocsDir
   156→    try {
   157→        git pull 2>&1 | Out-Null
   158→        Write-Log "  Updated." "Green"
   159→    } catch {
   160→        Write-Log "  Git pull failed, continuing..." "Yellow"
   161→    }
   162→    Pop-Location
   163→}
   164→
   165→if (-not (Test-Path $enterpriseDir)) {
   166→    Write-Host "[ERROR] No .claude/enterprise/ directory found. Repo may be outdated." -ForegroundColor Red
   167→    exit 1
   168→}
   169→
   170→Write-Log ""
   171→
   172→# ── Step 2: Create/verify user folder in repo ──────────────────────────
   173→
   174→Write-Log "[2/6] Setting up user folder in repository..." "Yellow"
   175→
   176→if (-not (Test-Path $userDir)) {
   177→    Write-Log "  Creating user folder: users/$Username/" "White"
   178→
   179→    # Create user directory structure
   180→    New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path (Join-Path $userDir "commands") -Force | Out-Null
   181→    New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path (Join-Path $userDir "skills") -Force | Out-Null
   182→    New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path (Join-Path $userDir "hooks") -Force | Out-Null
   183→
   184→    # Add .gitkeep files
   185→    "" | Set-Content (Join-Path $userDir "commands\.gitkeep")
   186→    "" | Set-Content (Join-Path $userDir "skills\.gitkeep")
   187→    "" | Set-Content (Join-Path $userDir "hooks\.gitkeep")
   188→
   189→    # Commit the new user folder
   190→    Push-Location $orgDocsDir
   191→    try {
   192→        git add ".claude/users/$Username"
   193→        git commit -m "feat: Add user folder for $Username`n`nCo-Authored-By: Claude Code <noreply@anthropic.com>" 2>&1 | Out-Null
   194→        git push 2>&1 | Out-Null
   195→        Write-Log "  Created and pushed user folder." "Green"
   196→    } catch {
   197→        Write-Log "  Created locally (push manually if needed)." "Yellow"
   198→    }
   199→    Pop-Location
   200→} else {
   201→    Write-Log "  User folder exists: users/$Username/" "Green"
   202→}
   203→
   204→Write-Log ""
   205→
   206→# ── Step 3: Create ~/.claude/ structure ────────────────────────────────
   207→
   208→Write-Log "[3/6] Creating ~/.claude/ directory structure..." "Yellow"
   209→
   210→if (-not (Test-Path $claudeDir)) {
   211→    New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $claudeDir -Force | Out-Null
   212→    Write-Log "  Created: $claudeDir" "Green"
   213→} else {
   214→    Write-Log "  Exists: $claudeDir" "Green"
   215→}
   216→
   217→# Local directories (user-specific state)
   218→$localDirs = @(
   219→    (Join-Path $claudeDir "state"),
   220→    (Join-Path $claudeDir "worktrees"),
   221→    (Join-Path $claudeDir "mcp-servers"),
   222→    (Join-Path $claudeDir "projects")
   223→)
   224→
   225→foreach ($dir in $localDirs) {
   226→    if (-not (Test-Path $dir)) {
   227→        New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $dir -Force | Out-Null
   228→    }
   229→}
   230→Write-Log "  Local state directories ready." "Green"
   231→
   232→Write-Log ""
   233→
   234→# ── Step 4: Create symlinks ────────────────────────────────────────────
   235→
   236→Write-Log "[4/6] Creating symlinks to enterprise + user content..." "Yellow"
   237→
   238→$created = 0
   239→$skipped = 0
   240→
   241→# Enterprise content symlinks (read from enterprise/, write to user/)
   242→$enterpriseLinks = @(
   243→    @{ Link = "commands-enterprise";  Target = (Join-Path $enterpriseDir "commands") },
   244→    @{ Link = "skills-enterprise";    Target = (Join-Path $enterpriseDir "skills") },
   245→    @{ Link = "reference";            Target = (Join-Path $enterpriseDir "reference") },
   246→    @{ Link = "learning";             Target = (Join-Path $enterpriseDir "learning") },
   247→    @{ Link = "agents";               Target = (Join-Path $enterpriseDir "agents") }
   248→)
   249→
   250→# User content symlinks
   251→$userLinks = @(
   252→    @{ Link = "commands-user"; Target = (Join-Path $userDir "commands") },
   253→    @{ Link = "skills-user";   Target = (Join-Path $userDir "skills") },
   254→    @{ Link = "hooks";         Target = (Join-Path $userDir "hooks") }
   255→)
   256→
   257→Write-Log "  Enterprise content (shared, read-only):" "White"
   258→foreach ($sl in $enterpriseLinks) {
   259→    $linkPath = Join-Path $claudeDir $sl.Link
   260→    if (Test-Path $sl.Target) {
   261→        if (New-Symlink -Link $linkPath -Target $sl.Target -Type "Junction") {
   262→            Write-Log "    $($sl.Link)/ -> enterprise/$($sl.Link -replace '-enterprise','')/" "Green"
   263→            $created++
   264→        } else { $skipped++ }
   265→    }
   266→}
   267→
   268→Write-Log "  User content (personal, read-write):" "White"
   269→foreach ($sl in $userLinks) {
   270→    $linkPath = Join-Path $claudeDir $sl.Link
   271→    if (Test-Path $sl.Target) {
   272→        if (New-Symlink -Link $linkPath -Target $sl.Target -Type "Junction") {
   273→            Write-Log "    $($sl.Link)/ -> users/$Username/$($sl.Link -replace '-user','')/" "Green"
   274→            $created++
   275→        } else { $skipped++ }
   276→    }
   277→}
   278→
   279→# CLAUDE.md - profile-specific or default
   280→$claudeMdSource = Join-Path $enterpriseDir "profiles\$UserType\CLAUDE.md"
   281→if (-not (Test-Path $claudeMdSource)) {
   282→    $claudeMdSource = Join-Path $enterpriseDir "CLAUDE.md"
   283→}
   284→$claudeMdLink = Join-Path $claudeDir "CLAUDE.md"
   285→
   286→if (Test-Path $claudeMdSource) {
   287→    if (New-Symlink -Link $claudeMdLink -Target $claudeMdSource -Type "SymbolicLink") {
   288→        Write-Log "    CLAUDE.md -> enterprise/CLAUDE.md" "Green"
   289→        $created++
   290→    } else { $skipped++ }
   291→}
   292→
   293→Write-Log "  Symlinks: $created created, $skipped skipped" "Green"
   294→Write-Log ""
   295→
   296→# ── Step 5: Generate MCP config ────────────────────────────────────────
   297→
   298→Write-Log "[5/6] Generating MCP configuration..." "Yellow"
   299→
   300→$mcpTemplatePath = Join-Path $sourceTemplates "mcp-config.json"
   301→$mcpDestPath = Join-Path $env:USERPROFILE ".mcp.json"
   302→
   303→if (Test-Path $mcpTemplatePath) {
   304→    $mcpContent = Get-Content $mcpTemplatePath -Raw
   305→    $userHomeEscaped = $env:USERPROFILE -replace '\\', '\\'
   306→    $mcpContent = $mcpContent -replace '\{\{USER_HOME\}\}', $userHomeEscaped
   307→
   308→    if ((Test-Path $mcpDestPath) -and -not $Force) {
   309→        Write-Log "  ~/.mcp.json exists (use -Force to overwrite)" "Yellow"
   310→    } else {
   311→        $mcpContent | Set-Content $mcpDestPath -Force
   312→        Write-Log "  Generated: ~/.mcp.json" "Green"
   313→    }
   314→
   315→    $mcpJson = $mcpContent | ConvertFrom-Json
   316→    $mcpCount = ($mcpJson.mcpServers.PSObject.Properties | Measure-Object).Count
   317→    Write-Log "  MCP servers: $mcpCount configured" "Green"
   318→} else {
   319→    Write-Log "  [!] MCP template not found" "Red"
   320→    $mcpCount = 0
   321→}
   322→
   323→# Settings symlink
   324→$settingsSource = Join-Path $sourceTemplates "settings.json"
   325→$settingsDest = Join-Path $claudeDir "settings.json"
   326→if (Test-Path $settingsSource) {
   327→    New-Symlink -Link $settingsDest -Target $settingsSource -Type "SymbolicLink" | Out-Null
   328→    Write-Log "  Linked: settings.json" "Green"
   329→}
   330→
   331→Write-Log ""
   332→
   333→# ── Step 6: Install auto-sync hook ─────────────────────────────────────
   334→
   335→Write-Log "[6/6] Installing auto-sync hook..." "Yellow"
   336→
   337→$hooksDir = Join-Path $claudeDir "hooks"
   338→if (-not (Test-Path $hooksDir)) {
   339→    New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $hooksDir -Force | Out-Null
   340→}
   341→
   342→# Create the sync script
   343→$syncScript = Join-Path $hooksDir "sync-org-docs.ps1"
   344→$syncContent = @"
   345→# Safari Circuits - Auto-sync hook
   346→# Pulls latest org-docs on Claude session start
   347→
   348→`$orgDocsDir = Join-Path `$env:USERPROFILE "organizational-docs"
   349→
   350→if (Test-Path `$orgDocsDir) {
   351→    Push-Location `$orgDocsDir
   352→    try {
   353→        `$result = git pull 2>&1
   354→        if (`$result -notmatch "Already up to date") {
   355→            Write-Host "[sync] Enterprise content updated" -ForegroundColor Green
   356→        }
   357→    } catch { }
   358→    Pop-Location
   359→}
   360→"@
   361→$syncContent | Set-Content $syncScript -Force
   362→
   363→Write-Log "  Created: sync-org-docs.ps1" "Green"
   364→Write-Log "  Auto-sync enabled for session start" "Green"
   365→Write-Log ""
   366→
   367→# ── Summary ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   368→
   369→Write-Log ("=" * 64) "Cyan"
   370→Write-Log "  Bootstrap Complete!" "Green"
   371→Write-Log ("=" * 64) "Cyan"
   372→Write-Log ""
   373→Write-Log "  Profile: $UserType" "White"
   374→Write-Log "  User folder: organizational-docs/.claude/users/$Username/" "White"
   375→Write-Log "  MCP servers: $mcpCount" "White"
   376→Write-Log "  Auto-sync: Enabled" "White"
   377→Write-Log ""
   378→Write-Log "  Content locations:" "Yellow"
   379→Write-Log "    Enterprise (shared):  ~/.claude/*-enterprise/" "White"
   380→Write-Log "    Personal (yours):     ~/.claude/*-user/" "White"
   381→Write-Log ""
   382→Write-Log "  Ready! Just run:" "Yellow"
   383→Write-Log ""
   384→Write-Log "    claude" "Cyan"
   385→Write-Log ""
   386→
   387→if ($UserType -eq "IT") {
   388→    Write-Log "  IT Commands: /start-work, /end-work, /groom, /branch, /pulse" "Gray"
   389→    Write-Log ""
   390→
   391→    # Check if Azure CLI is installed
   392→    $azInstalled = Get-Command az -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
   393→
   394→    if (-not $azInstalled) {
   395→        Write-Log "  [!] Azure CLI not installed - DevOps features will be limited" "Yellow"
   396→        Write-Log "      Install from: https://aka.ms/installazurecliwindows" "Yellow"
   397→    } else {
   398→        # Check Azure CLI login status
   399→        $azAccount = $null
   400→        try {
   401→            $azAccount = az account show 2>$null | ConvertFrom-Json
   402→        } catch { }
   403→
   404→        if ($azAccount) {
   405→            Write-Log "  Azure: Logged in as $($azAccount.user.name)" "Green"
   406→        } else {
   407→            Write-Log ""
   408→            Write-Log "  Signing into Azure for DevOps access..." "Yellow"
   409→            Write-Log "  (A browser will open - sign in with your Safari account)" "White"
   410→            Write-Log ""
   411→
   412→            # Run az login - this opens browser automatically
   413→            az login --allow-no-subscriptions 2>&1 | Out-Null
   414→
   415→            # Verify login worked
   416→            try {
   417→                $azAccount = az account show 2>$null | ConvertFrom-Json
   418→                if ($azAccount) {
   419→                    Write-Log "  Azure: Logged in as $($azAccount.user.name)" "Green"
   420→                }
   421→            } catch {
   422→                Write-Log "  [!] Azure login failed - DevOps features may not work" "Yellow"
   423→            }
   424→        }
   425→    }
   426→    Write-Log ""
   427→} else {
   428→    Write-Log "  Business Commands: /brain-dump, /reminder, /status" "Gray"
   429→}
   430→
   431→Write-Log ""
   432→
   433→# Write receipt
   434→$receipt = @{
   435→    version = "5.0"
   436→    profile = $UserType
   437→    username = $Username
   438→    bootstrapped = (Get-Date -Format "o")
   439→    orgDocsPath = $orgDocsDir
   440→    symlinksCreated = $created
   441→    mcpServers = $mcpCount
   442→}
   443→$receiptPath = Join-Path $claudeDir "state\bootstrap-receipt.json"
   444→$receipt | ConvertTo-Json | Set-Content $receiptPath -Force
   445→

1→# Safari Circuits - Claude Code One-Liner Install
     2→#
     3→# Usage (run this in PowerShell):
     4→#   irm https://raw.githubusercontent.com/SafariCircuitsLLC/organizational-docs/main/scripts/install.ps1 | iex
     5→#
     6→# What it does:
     7→#   1. Clones organizational-docs (if needed)
     8→#   2. Creates ~/.claude/ symlinks to enterprise content
     9→#   3. Generates ~/.mcp.json with browser-based auth
    10→#   4. Done - just run 'claude'
    11→
    12→$ErrorActionPreference = "Stop"
    13→
    14→Write-Host ""
    15→Write-Host "================================================" -ForegroundColor Cyan
    16→Write-Host "  Safari Circuits - Claude Code Setup" -ForegroundColor Cyan
    17→Write-Host "================================================" -ForegroundColor Cyan
    18→Write-Host ""
    19→
    20→# ── Check Prerequisites ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
    21→
    22→# Check for git
    23→if (-not (Get-Command git -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)) {
    24→    Write-Host "[ERROR] Git is not installed." -ForegroundColor Red
    25→    Write-Host "        Install from: https://git-scm.com/download/win" -ForegroundColor Yellow
    26→    exit 1
    27→}
    28→
    29→# Check for claude
    30→if (-not (Get-Command claude -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)) {
    31→    Write-Host "[ERROR] Claude Code is not installed." -ForegroundColor Red
    32→    Write-Host "        Install with: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code" -ForegroundColor Yellow
    33→    exit 1
    34→}
    35→
    36→# ── Clone or Update Repo ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
    37→
    38→$orgDocsDir = Join-Path $env:USERPROFILE "organizational-docs"
    39→
    40→if (Test-Path $orgDocsDir) {
    41→    Write-Host "[1/4] Updating organizational-docs..." -ForegroundColor Yellow
    42→    Push-Location $orgDocsDir
    43→    try {
    44→        git pull 2>&1 | Out-Null
    45→        Write-Host "      Updated to latest." -ForegroundColor Green
    46→    } catch {
    47→        Write-Host "      Warning: git pull failed, continuing..." -ForegroundColor Yellow
    48→    }
    49→    Pop-Location
    50→} else {
    51→    Write-Host "[1/4] Cloning organizational-docs..." -ForegroundColor Yellow
    52→    Push-Location $env:USERPROFILE
    53→    try {
    54→        git clone "https://github.com/SafariCircuitsLLC/organizational-docs.git" 2>&1 | Out-Null
    55→        Write-Host "      Cloned successfully." -ForegroundColor Green
    56→    } catch {
    57→        Write-Host "[ERROR] Failed to clone. Check your GitHub access." -ForegroundColor Red
    58→        Pop-Location
    59→        exit 1
    60→    }
    61→    Pop-Location
    62→}
    63→
    64→# ── Create ~/.claude/ Structure ──────────────────────────────────────────
    65→
    66→Write-Host "[2/4] Setting up ~/.claude/ directory..." -ForegroundColor Yellow
    67→
    68→$claudeDir = Join-Path $env:USERPROFILE ".claude"
    69→$enterpriseDir = Join-Path $orgDocsDir ".claude\enterprise"
    70→
    71→# Create base directory
    72→if (-not (Test-Path $claudeDir)) {
    73→    New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $claudeDir -Force | Out-Null
    74→}
    75→
    76→# Create local state directories
    77→@("state", "projects", "mcp-servers") | ForEach-Object {
    78→    $dir = Join-Path $claudeDir $_
    79→    if (-not (Test-Path $dir)) {
    80→        New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $dir -Force | Out-Null
    81→    }
    82→}
    83→
    84→Write-Host "      Directory structure ready." -ForegroundColor Green
    85→
    86→# ── Create Symlinks ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    87→
    88→Write-Host "[3/4] Creating symlinks to enterprise content..." -ForegroundColor Yellow
    89→
    90→$symlinks = @(
    91→    @{ Name = "commands"; Target = "commands" },
    92→    @{ Name = "skills"; Target = "skills" },
    93→    @{ Name = "reference"; Target = "reference" },
    94→    @{ Name = "learning"; Target = "learning" },
    95→    @{ Name = "agents"; Target = "agents" }
    96→)
    97→
    98→$created = 0
    99→foreach ($link in $symlinks) {
   100→    $linkPath = Join-Path $claudeDir $link.Name
   101→    $targetPath = Join-Path $enterpriseDir $link.Target
   102→
   103→    if (Test-Path $targetPath) {
   104→        if (Test-Path $linkPath) {
   105→            Remove-Item $linkPath -Force -Recurse -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
   106→        }
   107→
   108→        try {
   109→            # Use cmd mklink for compatibility (works without admin for junctions)
   110→            cmd /c mklink /J "$linkPath" "$targetPath" 2>&1 | Out-Null
   111→            $created++
   112→        } catch {
   113→            Write-Host "      Warning: Failed to create $($link.Name) symlink" -ForegroundColor Yellow
   114→        }
   115→    }
   116→}
   117→
   118→# CLAUDE.md symlink (file, not directory)
   119→$claudeMdTarget = Join-Path $enterpriseDir "CLAUDE.md"
   120→$claudeMdLink = Join-Path $claudeDir "CLAUDE.md"
   121→
   122→if (Test-Path $claudeMdTarget) {
   123→    if (Test-Path $claudeMdLink) {
   124→        Remove-Item $claudeMdLink -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
   125→    }
   126→    try {
   127→        cmd /c mklink "$claudeMdLink" "$claudeMdTarget" 2>&1 | Out-Null
   128→        $created++
   129→    } catch {
   130→        # File symlinks may need admin - try copy as fallback
   131→        Copy-Item $claudeMdTarget $claudeMdLink -Force
   132→        $created++
   133→    }
   134→}
   135→
   136→Write-Host "      Created $created symlinks." -ForegroundColor Green
   137→
   138→# ── Generate MCP Config ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
   139→
   140→Write-Host "[4/4] Generating MCP configuration..." -ForegroundColor Yellow
   141→
   142→$mcpTemplatePath = Join-Path $orgDocsDir ".claude\templates\mcp-config.json"
   143→$mcpDestPath = Join-Path $env:USERPROFILE ".mcp.json"
   144→
   145→if (Test-Path $mcpTemplatePath) {
   146→    $mcpContent = Get-Content $mcpTemplatePath -Raw
   147→    $userHomeEscaped = $env:USERPROFILE -replace '\\', '\\'
   148→    $mcpContent = $mcpContent -replace '\{\{USER_HOME\}\}', $userHomeEscaped
   149→    $mcpContent | Set-Content $mcpDestPath -Force
   150→
   151→    $mcpJson = $mcpContent | ConvertFrom-Json
   152→    $mcpCount = ($mcpJson.mcpServers.PSObject.Properties | Measure-Object).Count
   153→    Write-Host "      Configured $mcpCount MCP servers." -ForegroundColor Green
   154→} else {
   155→    Write-Host "      Warning: MCP template not found." -ForegroundColor Yellow
   156→}
   157→
   158→# ── Done ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   159→
   160→Write-Host ""
   161→Write-Host "================================================" -ForegroundColor Green
   162→Write-Host "  Setup Complete!" -ForegroundColor Green
   163→Write-Host "================================================" -ForegroundColor Green
   164→Write-Host ""
   165→Write-Host "  Just run:" -ForegroundColor White
   166→Write-Host ""
   167→Write-Host "    claude" -ForegroundColor Cyan
   168→Write-Host ""
   169→Write-Host "  First DevOps command will open browser to authenticate." -ForegroundColor Yellow
   170→Write-Host ""
   171→

1→# Unified DevOps Workflow (GitHub + Azure DevOps + Claude Code)
     2→
     3→Maintain consistency across all three systems for seamless work tracking.
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Single Source of Truth Hierarchy
     8→
     9→```
    10→Azure DevOps Work Items (WHY & WHAT)
    11→    ↓
    12→GitHub Branches & PRs (HOW)
    13→    ↓
    14→Claude Code Sessions (EXECUTION)
    15→```
    16→
    17→---
    18→
    19→## Naming Conventions
    20→
    21→| System | Pattern | Example |
    22→|--------|---------|---------|
    23→| Azure DevOps Task | `[Action]: [Description]` | `Implement: GSD context checkpoints` |
    24→| GitHub Branch | `[type]/[work-item-id]-[slug]` | `feature/1001-gsd-checkpoints` |
    25→| Git Commit | `[type]: [description] (#[work-item])` | `feat: add checkpoint command (#1001)` |
    26→| Claude Session | `[work-item-id] - [focus]` | `#1001 - Implementing GSD checkpoints` |
    27→
    28→---
    29→
    30→## Branch Types
    31→
    32→| Type | Use Case |
    33→|------|----------|
    34→| `feature/` | New functionality |
    35→| `fix/` | Bug fixes |
    36→| `refactor/` | Code improvements |
    37→| `docs/` | Documentation only |
    38→| `config/` | Configuration changes |
    39→
    40→---
    41→
    42→## Workflow Sync Points
    43→
    44→| Event | Azure DevOps | GitHub | Claude Code |
    45→|-------|--------------|--------|-------------|
    46→| Start work | Task → Active | Create branch | `/start-work` links work item |
    47→| Checkpoint | Add comment | N/A | `/checkpoint` documents progress |
    48→| Code complete | N/A | Create PR | PR linked to work item |
    49→| PR merged | Task → Closed | Branch deleted | `/end-work` closes task |
    50→
    51→---
    52→
    53→## Auto-Linking Protocol
    54→
    55→**On `/start-work`:**
    56→1. Query active Azure DevOps work items
    57→2. User selects or creates work item
    58→3. Create GitHub branch using naming convention
    59→4. Store work item ID in session state
    60→
    61→**On commit:**
    62→1. Include work item ID in commit message: `feat: description (#1001)`
    63→2. Azure DevOps auto-links commit via hashtag
    64→
    65→**On PR creation:**
    66→1. Link PR to work item via `AB#1001` in description
    67→2. PR title follows: `[Type]: Description (#1001)`
    68→
    69→**On `/end-work`:**
    70→1. Update work item state (Active → Closed)
    71→2. Add completion summary with AI metrics
    72→3. Link commits to work item
    73→
    74→---
    75→
    76→## Phase Tracking Integration
    77→
    78→| Claude Phase | Azure DevOps | GitHub |
    79→|--------------|--------------|--------|
    80→| Phase start | Add work item comment | N/A |
    81→| Phase checkpoint | Update task progress | Push commits |
    82→| Phase complete | Close sub-task | Squash merge ready |
    83→
    84→---
    85→
    86→## Consistency Checks
    87→
    88→Run periodically or on `/status`:
    89→
    90→```
    91→- Work item linked: [yes/no]
    92→- Branch naming: [valid/invalid]
    93→- Commits linked: [N commits]
    94→- PR status: [none/draft/ready/merged]
    95→```
    96→
    97→---
    98→
    99→## Tag Taxonomy
   100→
   101→Standardized tags enable consistent search, filtering, and reporting across all work items.
   102→
   103→### Tag Categories
   104→
   105→| Category | Convention | Examples | Purpose |
   106→|----------|------------|----------|---------|
   107→| **Product** | `lowercase-hyphen` | `safari-trace`, `safari-assist`, `safari-analyze`, `safari-studio` | Link work to Safari product suite |
   108→| **Phase** | `phase-N` | `phase-1`, `phase-2`, `phase-6` | Track implementation phases |
   109→| **Domain** | `lowercase` | `manufacturing`, `finance`, `security`, `infrastructure` | Business domain classification |
   110→| **System** | `PascalCase` | `PLEX`, `BeFirst`, `Planful`, `Salesforce`, `FortiGate` | External system integration |
   111→| **Technology** | `lowercase-hyphen` | `nextjs`, `kafka`, `azure-ad`, `power-bi`, `spark` | Technology stack |
   112→| **Work Type** | `lowercase-hyphen` | `integration`, `etl`, `cdc`, `dashboard`, `sso`, `api` | Type of technical work |
   113→| **Status** | `lowercase` | `urgent`, `blocked` | Priority/status flags |
   114→| **Strategic** | `lowercase-hyphen` | `it-roadmap`, `ai-operations` | Strategic initiative alignment |
   115→| **Site** | `UPPERCASE` | `GRA1`, `OTS`, `JUA` | Physical site location |
   116→
   117→### Tag Examples by Work Type
   118→
   119→| Work Item Type | Typical Tags |
   120→|----------------|--------------|
   121→| Data integration | `etl`, `kafka`, `PLEX`, `manufacturing` |
   122→| Security project | `security`, `FortiGate`, `azure-ad`, `phase-1` |
   123→| Dashboard work | `dashboard`, `power-bi`, `finance`, `Planful` |
   124→| Product feature | `safari-trace`, `nextjs`, `api`, `phase-2` |
   125→
   126→### Tag Governance Rules
   127→
   128→1. **Always use existing tags** - Check for existing tags before creating new ones
   129→2. **No spaces** - Use hyphens for multi-word tags: `azure-ad` not `azure ad`
   130→3. **Lowercase default** - Except for System (PascalCase) and Site (UPPERCASE)
   131→4. **Minimum tags** - Every work item should have at least: domain + work-type
   132→5. **No duplicates** - Avoid synonyms: use `dashboard` not `report` and `dashboard`
   133→
   134→### Archive Area Path
   135→
   136→Items moved to `Information Technology\Archive` are excluded from active queries. Use for:
   137→- Consolidated/superseded work items
   138→- Mass-uploaded items that won't be actioned
   139→- Completed work older than 90 days
   140→
   141→Run periodic cleanup script to purge archived items per retention policy.
   142→

1→# DevOps Auto-Sync
     2→
     3→Automatic work tracking and Azure DevOps synchronization. **Always-on for IT profile** - runs silently throughout every session.
     4→
     5→## Purpose
     6→
     7→Makes DevOps tracking invisible - Claude automatically infers, tracks, and syncs work to Azure DevOps without users thinking about it.
     8→
     9→**Key Principle:** Users should NEVER have to say "track this in DevOps" or manually create work items. Work is captured automatically.
    10→
    11→## Operating Mode (ENFORCED)
    12→
    13→| Profile | Mode | Behavior |
    14→|---------|------|----------|
    15→| **IT** | **Always-On** | Inference runs every checkpoint; work items auto-created |
    16→| **Business** | Off | DevOps tracking disabled |
    17→
    18→## When This Runs
    19→
    20→| Trigger | Action |
    21→|---------|--------|
    22→| **Session start** | Initialize work-tracker.json, detect orphaned sessions |
    23→| **Every 10 turns** | Light inference scan (high-confidence signals only) |
    24→| **Checkpoint** (~40 turns) | Full inference + sync to ADO |
    25→| **Task completion detected** | Update work items, add summaries |
    26→| **Topic shift detected** | Infer and queue new work item |
    27→| **`/sync-devops`** | Force immediate sync |
    28→| **`/end-work`** | Full sync with work item creation/closure |
    29→
    30→## Always-On Tracking Flow
    31→
    32→```
    33→SESSION_START:
    34→  1. Initialize work-tracker.json with new session
    35→  2. Set sessionStartTime, activeWork = []
    36→  3. Load DevOps MCP tools (IT profile only)
    37→
    38→EVERY_10_TURNS:
    39→  1. Scan last 10 turns for work signals
    40→  2. If high-confidence signal (>= 0.85):
    41→     - Add to activeWork queue
    42→     - Show brief acknowledgment: "📋 Tracking: [inferred title]"
    43→  3. Update work-tracker.json
    44→
    45→CHECKPOINT (40 turns):
    46→  1. Full inference on session so far
    47→  2. Create ADO items for >= 0.90 confidence
    48→  3. Queue 0.70-0.89 for end-work review
    49→  4. Add progress comments to active items
    50→
    51→END_WORK:
    52→  1. Final inference pass
    53→  2. Create all queued items >= 0.70 (with brief confirm)
    54→  3. Close completed items
    55→  4. Link commits to work items
    56→```
    57→
    58→## Inline Acknowledgments
    59→
    60→When work is detected during a session, show a brief inline acknowledgment:
    61→
    62→```
    63→📋 Tracking: "Fix authentication timeout bug" (Bug, 0.92)
    64→```
    65→
    66→This confirms tracking is happening without interrupting flow. Users can say "don't track [topic]" to exclude.
    67→
    68→---
    69→
    70→## Work Inference Engine
    71→
    72→### Signal Detection
    73→
    74→Scan recent conversation (last 20 turns) for these patterns:
    75→
    76→| Signal | Patterns | Work Type | Base Confidence |
    77→|--------|----------|-----------|-----------------|
    78→| **Bug** | "bug", "error", "fix", "broken", stack traces, error messages | Bug | 0.80 |
    79→| **Feature** | "implement", "add", "new feature", "create" | Task/Requirement | 0.70 |
    80→| **Research** | "investigate", "spike", "POC", "research", "explore" | Research Task | 0.70 |
    81→| **Documentation** | .md file edits, "document", "README", "docs" | Documentation Task | 0.70 |
    82→| **Refactor** | "refactor", "cleanup", "improve", "optimize" | Task (refactor) | 0.60 |
    83→| **Config** | MCP setup, .json edits, "configure" | Task (config) | 0.60 |
    84→
    85→### Confidence Scoring
    86→
    87→```
    88→confidence = base_score + signal_strength + context_bonus
    89→
    90→base_score: From signal detection table above
    91→
    92→signal_strength:
    93→  +0.10 per additional matching signal
    94→  +0.20 if stack trace or error message present (for bugs)
    95→
    96→context_bonus:
    97→  +0.20 if matches active Feature under Epic #974
    98→  +0.10 if matches current iteration work
    99→  +0.30 if user explicitly confirms ("yes track this")
   100→```
   101→
   102→### Confidence Thresholds
   103→
   104→| Confidence | Action |
   105→|------------|--------|
   106→| >= 0.90 | **Auto-sync** - Create/update ADO silently |
   107→| 0.70 - 0.89 | **Suggest** - "Shall I track this as [Type]?" |
   108→| 0.50 - 0.69 | **Queue** - Add to checkpoint summary for review |
   109→| < 0.50 | **Ignore** - Not enough signal |
   110→
   111→### Work Type Mapping
   112→
   113→```
   114→FUNCTION infer_work_type(signals, context):
   115→
   116→  # Bug detection (highest priority)
   117→  IF signals.has("bug", "error", "fix", "broken") OR signals.has_stack_trace:
   118→    IF signals.has_code_change:
   119→      RETURN Bug (confidence: 0.90)
   120→    ELSE:
   121→      RETURN Bug (confidence: 0.70)
   122→
   123→  # Feature work
   124→  IF signals.has("implement", "add", "new feature", "create"):
   125→    IF context.matches_active_feature:
   126→      RETURN Task under matched Feature (confidence: 0.85)
   127→    ELSE:
   128→      RETURN Requirement (confidence: 0.70)
   129→
   130→  # Research
   131→  IF signals.has("investigate", "spike", "research", "POC", "explore"):
   132→    RETURN Task (activity: Research) (confidence: 0.75)
   133→
   134→  # Documentation
   135→  IF signals.file_types.only_markdown OR signals.has("document", "docs"):
   136→    RETURN Task (activity: Documentation) (confidence: 0.75)
   137→
   138→  # Refactoring
   139→  IF signals.has("refactor", "cleanup", "improve", "optimize"):
   140→    RETURN Task (activity: Development) (confidence: 0.70)
   141→
   142→  # Code changes without explicit type
   143→  IF signals.has_code_change AND context.has_active_work_item:
   144→    RETURN link_to_active_work_item (confidence: 0.85)
   145→
   146→  # Insufficient signals
   147→  RETURN null
   148→```
   149→
   150→---
   151→
   152→## State Management
   153→
   154→### work-tracker.json Structure
   155→
   156→Located at `~/.claude/state/work-tracker.json`:
   157→
   158→```json
   159→{
   160→  "version": "1.0",
   161→  "currentSession": {
   162→    "id": "2026-01-19-03",
   163→    "parentSession": "2026-01-19-02",
   164→    "startTime": "2026-01-19T15:00:00Z",
   165→    "lastSync": "2026-01-19T16:30:00Z",
   166→    "completed": false,
   167→    "checkpointCount": 2
   168→  },
   169→  "activeWork": [
   170→    {
   171→      "inferenceId": "inf-001",
   172→      "title": "Fix authentication timeout bug",
   173→      "type": "Bug",
   174→      "confidence": 0.92,
   175→      "signals": ["error discussed", "code change", "stack trace"],
   176→      "firstDetected": "2026-01-19T15:15:00Z",
   177→      "adoId": null,
   178→      "synced": false,
   179→      "status": "pending-create",
   180→      "parentFeature": 977,
   181→      "files": ["src/auth/session.ts"]
   182→    }
   183→  ],
   184→  "rejectedInferences": [
   185→    {
   186→      "inferenceId": "inf-003",
   187→      "title": "General architecture discussion",
   188→      "reason": "user-rejected",
   189→      "rejectedAt": "2026-01-19T16:00:00Z",
   190→      "cooldownUntil": "2026-01-20T16:00:00Z"
   191→    }
   192→  ],
   193→  "syncHistory": [
   194→    {
   195→      "timestamp": "2026-01-19T16:30:00Z",
   196→      "type": "checkpoint",
   197→      "itemsSynced": 1,
   198→      "itemsCreated": 0,
   199→      "commentsAdded": 1
   200→    }
   201→  ],
   202→  "sessionHistory": []
   203→}
   204→```
   205→
   206→---
   207→
   208→## Checkpoint Sync Flow
   209→
   210→### When Checkpoint Triggers
   211→
   212→Run this sync as part of `/checkpoint` (silently):
   213→
   214→### Steps
   215→
   216→```
   217→CHECKPOINT_SYNC():
   218→  1. Read work-tracker.json
   219→  2. Read session.json for activeWorkItems
   220→  3. Get git diff/status if in repo
   221→
   222→  4. For each item in activeWork:
   223→
   224→     IF item.confidence >= 0.90 AND item.adoId == null:
   225→       # Auto-create in ADO
   226→       result = wit_create_work_item(
   227→         project: "Information Technology",
   228→         workItemType: item.type,
   229→         fields: [
   230→           {name: "System.Title", value: item.title},
   231→           {name: "System.AreaPath", value: "Information Technology\\Software Engineering"},
   232→           {name: "System.State", value: "Active"}
   233→         ]
   234→       )
   235→       item.adoId = result.id
   236→       item.synced = true
   237→       item.status = "linked"
   238→
   239→     ELIF item.adoId != null AND NOT recently_synced(item):
   240→       # Add progress comment
   241→       wit_add_work_item_comment(
   242→         workItemId: item.adoId,
   243→         project: "Information Technology",
   244→         comment: generate_progress_comment(item),
   245→         format: "markdown"
   246→       )
   247→       item.lastSyncedComment = now()
   248→
   249→  5. Update work-tracker.json
   250→
   251→  6. Add to syncHistory:
   252→     {timestamp, type: "checkpoint", itemsSynced, itemsCreated, commentsAdded}
   253→```
   254→
   255→### Progress Comment Format
   256→
   257→```markdown
   258→**[CHECKPOINT-SYNC]**
   259→Session: {sessionId}
   260→Checkpoint: #{checkpointNumber}
   261→
   262→**Progress:**
   263→- {accomplishments since last sync}
   264→
   265→**Files Modified:**
   266→- {file list}
   267→
   268→**Context Health:** {Fresh|Warm|Hot}
   269→```
   270→
   271→---
   272→
   273→## Session Recovery
   274→
   275→### Orphan Detection (runs on /start-work)
   276→
   277→An orphaned session is detected when:
   278→- `session.json` has `completed: false`
   279→- AND `lastActivity` is > 4 hours ago
   280→- AND `work-tracker.json` has unsynced items
   281→
   282→### Recovery Flow
   283→
   284→```
   285→SESSION_RECOVERY():
   286→  1. Read session.json
   287→  2. Read work-tracker.json
   288→
   289→  3. IF session.completed == false:
   290→     idle_hours = hours_since(session.lastActivity)
   291→
   292→     IF idle_hours >= 4:
   293→       unsynced = work-tracker.activeWork.filter(synced == false)
   294→
   295→       SHOW:
   296→       ```
   297→       ⚠️ **Previous session not properly closed**
   298→
   299→       **Session**: {session.sessionId}
   300→       **Focus**: {session.focus}
   301→       **Last Active**: {session.lastActivity} ({idle_hours}h ago)
   302→       **Unsynced Work**: {unsynced.length} items
   303→
   304→       **Options:**
   305→       1. **Close previous** - Sync work items to ADO, then start fresh
   306→       2. **Continue previous** - Resume where you left off
   307→       3. **Discard** - Archive without syncing, start fresh
   308→       ```
   309→
   310→       WAIT for user choice
   311→
   312→       IF choice == "1" (Close):
   313→         RUN full end-work sync for orphaned session
   314→         ARCHIVE session to ~/.claude/state/archived-sessions/
   315→         START fresh session
   316→
   317→       IF choice == "2" (Continue):
   318→         RESUME session
   319→         UPDATE lastActivity
   320→
   321→       IF choice == "3" (Discard):
   322→         ARCHIVE session without sync
   323→         START fresh session
   324→```
   325→
   326→### Archive Format
   327→
   328→Archive orphaned sessions to `~/.claude/state/archived-sessions/{sessionId}.json`:
   329→
   330→```json
   331→{
   332→  "sessionId": "2026-01-19-02",
   333→  "archived": "2026-01-19T18:00:00Z",
   334→  "reason": "orphan-recovered",
   335→  "syncedBeforeArchive": true,
   336→  "workItems": [...],
   337→  "originalSession": {...}
   338→}
   339→```
   340→
   341→---
   342→
   343→## Enforcement Rules
   344→
   345→### MUST Track (Mandatory - Auto-create)
   346→
   347→| Work Type | Trigger | Confidence Required |
   348→|-----------|---------|---------------------|
   349→| Bug fixes | Error + code change | >= 0.90 (auto) |
   350→| Feature completion | "done", "finished", "shipped" | >= 0.90 (auto) |
   351→
   352→### SHOULD Track (Recommended - Suggest)
   353→
   354→| Work Type | Trigger | Confidence Required |
   355→|-----------|---------|---------------------|
   356→| Research spikes | Investigation keywords | >= 0.70 (suggest) |
   357→| Documentation | .md file changes | >= 0.70 (suggest) |
   358→| Refactoring | Refactor keywords + code | >= 0.70 (suggest) |
   359→
   360→### OPTIONAL Track (User Discretion)
   361→
   362→| Work Type | Trigger | Action |
   363→|-----------|---------|--------|
   364→| Minor fixes | Small tweaks | Show in checkpoint summary |
   365→| Exploration | Questions, learning | Do not track |
   366→| Conversation | Discussion only | Do not track |
   367→
   368→---
   369→
   370→## False Positive Prevention
   371→
   372→To minimize unwanted work item creation:
   373→
   374→1. **Context Window**: Only analyze last 20 turns (not entire session)
   375→2. **Confirmation Required**: Below 0.90 confidence, always ask
   376→3. **Explicit Opt-Out**: "don't track this" prevents inference for topic
   377→4. **Domain Filtering**: Only infer work related to active Epic/Features
   378→5. **Cooldown**: Rejected items won't be re-suggested for 24 hours
   379→6. **No Duplicates**: Check existing ADO items before suggesting new
   380→
   381→### Opt-Out Handling
   382→
   383→When user says "don't track this":
   384→
   385→```
   386→1. Add to rejectedInferences:
   387→   {
   388→     inferenceId: generate_id(),
   389→     title: inferred_title,
   390→     reason: "user-rejected",
   391→     rejectedAt: now(),
   392→     cooldownUntil: now() + 24hr
   393→   }
   394→
   395→2. Remove from activeWork if present
   396→
   397→3. Confirm: "Got it - won't track this topic"
   398→```
   399→
   400→---
   401→
   402→## Commands
   403→
   404→### `/sync-devops` - Force Sync
   405→
   406→Force immediate sync to Azure DevOps:
   407→
   408→```
   409→SYNC_DEVOPS():
   410→  1. Run full checkpoint sync
   411→  2. Show sync summary:
   412→     ```
   413→     **DevOps Sync Complete**
   414→     - Items synced: {count}
   415→     - Items created: {count}
   416→     - Comments added: {count}
   417→
   418→     **Active Work Items:**
   419→     - #{id}: {title} (confidence: {conf})
   420→     ```
   421→```
   422→
   423→### `/inferred-work` - Show Inferred Items
   424→
   425→Display what Claude thinks you're working on:
   426→
   427→```
   428→INFERRED_WORK():
   429→  Read work-tracker.json
   430→
   431→  SHOW:
   432→  ```
   433→  **Inferred Work Items**
   434→
   435→  | # | Title | Type | Confidence | ADO ID | Status |
   436→  |---|-------|------|------------|--------|--------|
   437→  | inf-001 | Fix auth timeout | Bug | 0.92 | #1001 | synced |
   438→  | inf-002 | Add checkpoint sync | Task | 0.85 | - | pending |
   439→
   440→  **Commands:**
   441→  - "track this as #123" - Link to existing work item
   442→  - "don't track [item]" - Remove from tracking
   443→  - `/sync-devops` - Force sync now
   444→  ```
   445→```
   446→
   447→### `/reject-inference [id]` - Reject Inference
   448→
   449→Reject a specific inferred item:
   450→
   451→```
   452→REJECT_INFERENCE(inference_id):
   453→  1. Find item in activeWork
   454→  2. Move to rejectedInferences with 24hr cooldown
   455→  3. Confirm: "Removed {title} from tracking"
   456→```
   457→
   458→---
   459→
   460→## Integration Points
   461→
   462→### With /checkpoint
   463→
   464→Add to checkpoint.md after step 2 (Update Session State):
   465→
   466→```markdown
   467→### 2.5. Sync to DevOps (SILENT)
   468→
   469→Run the DevOps sync flow:
   470→1. Read work-tracker.json
   471→2. For high-confidence items: auto-create or update
   472→3. For medium-confidence items: queue for checkpoint summary
   473→4. Update work-tracker.json with sync status
   474→
   475→This runs silently unless there are items needing user confirmation.
   476→```
   477→
   478→### With /start-work
   479→
   480→Add to start-work.md before step 1:
   481→
   482→```markdown
   483→### 0. Session Recovery Check
   484→
   485→Check for orphaned previous sessions:
   486→1. Read session.json and work-tracker.json
   487→2. If previous session not completed AND idle > 4 hours:
   488→   - Show orphan warning with options
   489→   - Wait for user choice
   490→   - Execute recovery action
   491→```
   492→
   493→### With /end-work
   494→
   495→Enhanced sync in step 9 (Update Azure DevOps):
   496→
   497→```markdown
   498→### 9. Comprehensive DevOps Sync
   499→
   500→Use work-tracker.json for complete sync:
   501→1. Close all completed inferred items
   502→2. Add final summaries to all active items
   503→3. Link commits to relevant work items
   504→4. Archive session to sessionHistory
   505→5. Reset activeWork for next session
   506→```
   507→
   508→---
   509→
   510→## Related
   511→
   512→- `/checkpoint` - Triggers checkpoint sync
   513→- `/start-work` - Includes session recovery
   514→- `/end-work` - Full sync with all items
   515→- `/reminder` - Items feed into inference engine
   516→- `/status` - Shows inferred work count
   517→

     2→id: DOC-IT-ADO-001
     3→title: Azure DevOps Process Guide
     4→domain: operations
     5→visualization: process
     6→sensitivity: internal
     7→last_updated: 2026-02-10
     8→---
     9→
    10→# Azure DevOps Process Guide
    11→
    12→How the Safari Circuits IT team manages work using Azure DevOps, Claude Code, and a CMMI + Kanban hybrid workflow.
    13→
    14→## Project Overview
    15→
    16→| Setting | Value |
    17→|---------|-------|
    18→| Organization | SafariCircuitsLLC |
    19→| Project | Information Technology |
    20→| URL | https://dev.azure.com/SafariCircuitsLLC/Information%20Technology |
    21→| Process Template | CMMI |
    22→| Primary Workflow | Kanban (continuous flow) |
    23→| Planned Work | Quarterly iterations when needed |
    24→
    25→## Process Model: CMMI + Kanban Hybrid
    26→
    27→We use the **CMMI process template** for governance and traceability, combined with **Kanban flow** for daily work management.
    28→
    29→- **Kanban (default)**: Work items flow through board columns (Proposed > Active > Resolved > Closed). No time-boxed sprints. Pull work when capacity opens.
    30→- **Quarterly iterations**: Used for planned project milestones (e.g., Safari platform phases, major integrations). Assign items to Q1/Q2/etc. when planning project work.
    31→- **No mandatory sprint ceremonies**: Stand-ups and retrospectives happen organically, not on a forced cadence.
    32→
    33→### When to Use Iterations vs. Kanban
    34→
    35→| Work Type | Approach | Iteration |
    36→|-----------|----------|-----------|
    37→| Ad-hoc tasks, incidents, support | Kanban flow | Backlog (or none) |
    38→| Planned project phases | Assign to quarter | Q1 2026, Q2 2026, etc. |
    39→| Infrastructure provisioning | Kanban flow | Backlog |
    40→| Feature development sprints | Assign to quarter | Quarterly iteration |
    41→
    42→## Team Structure
    43→
    44→Five teams map to IT subdomains. Each team has a matching Area Path.
    45→
    46→| Team | Area Path | Focus |
    47→|------|-----------|-------|
    48→| Software Engineering | `Information Technology\Software Engineering` | App dev, integrations, DevOps |
    49→| Data Services | `Information Technology\Data Services` | ETL, analytics, data governance, BI |
    50→| Infrastructure Management | `Information Technology\Infrastructure Management` | Servers, networking, cloud, hardware |
    51→| Cybersecurity | `Information Technology\Cybersecurity` | Security ops, identity, compliance |
    52→| Help Desk and Support | `Information Technology\Help Desk and Support` | ITSM, ticketing, user support |
    53→
    54→**Special Area Paths:**
    55→- `Information Technology\Archive` - Retired/superseded items (excluded from active queries)
    56→- `Information Technology` (root) - Avoid; assign to a specific team
    57→
    58→## Work Item Hierarchy
    59→
    60→```
    61→Epic (strategic initiative)
    62→  Feature (deliverable capability)
    63→    Requirement (what needs to be true)
    64→      Task (actionable work unit)
    65→```
    66→
    67→CMMI also provides: Change Request, Issue, Bug, Risk, Review
    68→
    69→## Work Item Lifecycle
    70→
    71→### States (CMMI)
    72→
    73→```
    74→Proposed ──> Active ──> Resolved ──> Closed
    75→                           │
    76→                    (Tasks skip this)
    77→```
    78→
    79→### Governance Tags
    80→
    81→| State | Tag | Meaning | Next Action |
    82→|-------|-----|---------|-------------|
    83→| Proposed | *(none)* | Backlog item — raw, not yet refined | Groom when prioritized |
    84→| Proposed | `Groomed` | Well-defined — has description, criteria, tags (not approval) | Pull into Active |
    85→| Active | *(any)* | In progress | Complete the work |
    86→| Resolved | *(none)* | Dev complete — needs business validation | Business reviews deliverable |
    87→| Resolved | `Validated` | Business sign-off — stakeholder confirmed it works | Close |
    88→| Closed | *(any)* | Done and accepted | Archive after 90 days |
    89→
    90→### Rules
    91→
    92→1. **Create items as Proposed** - Everything starts in the backlog
    93→2. **Only Groomed items get pulled** - Don't start work on items that aren't well-defined
    94→3. **Groomed means well-defined, not approved** - Grooming checks input quality (description, criteria, tags). Business sign-off happens at the Validation gate after work is complete.
    95→4. **Tasks skip Resolved** - Go directly from Active to Closed
    96→5. **Tasks require Completion Summary** - Set `Custom.CompletionSummary` when closing tasks
    97→6. **Never auto-close** - Leave at Resolved for business validation (the actual sign-off)
    98→
    99→## Tag Taxonomy
   100→
   101→Tags enable classification and analytics. Claude enforces tag standards at the point of work item creation.
   102→
   103→### Tag Categories
   104→
   105→| Category | Convention | Examples | Purpose |
   106→|----------|------------|----------|---------|
   107→| **Domain** | `lowercase` | `manufacturing`, `finance`, `security`, `infrastructure` | Business domain |
   108→| **System** | `PascalCase` | `PLEX`, `BeFirst`, `Planful`, `Salesforce`, `FortiGate` | External system |
   109→| **Technology** | `lowercase-hyphen` | `nextjs`, `kafka`, `azure-ad`, `power-bi`, `spark` | Tech stack |
   110→| **Work Type** | `lowercase-hyphen` | `integration`, `etl`, `cdc`, `dashboard`, `sso`, `api` | Type of work |
   111→| **Phase** | `phase-N` | `phase-1`, `phase-6` | Implementation phase |
   112→| **Product** | `lowercase-hyphen` | `safari-trace`, `safari-assist`, `safari-platform` | Safari product suite |
   113→| **Site** | `UPPERCASE` | `GRA1`, `OTS`, `JUA` | Physical location |
   114→| **Skill** | `skill:name` | `skill:networking`, `skill:data-engineering`, `skill:security` | Resource/skill classification |
   115→| **Status** | `lowercase` | `urgent`, `blocked`, `Groomed`, `Validated` | Priority/governance flags |
   116→
   117→### Tag Rules
   118→
   119→1. Every work item needs at least: **domain + work-type**
   120→2. Use hyphens for multi-word tags: `azure-ad` not `azure ad`
   121→3. Check existing tags before creating new ones
   122→4. Skill tags use the `skill:` prefix for filterability
   123→5. Claude validates tags on creation; daily audit catches drift
   124→
   125→### Tag-Based Analytics
   126→
   127→Tags power resource and capacity reporting:
   128→
   129→- **Domain distribution**: Where is work concentrated? (manufacturing vs. finance vs. security)
   130→- **Skill gaps**: What `skill:` tags appear most without assigned resources?
   131→- **System workload**: Which external systems generate the most work items?
   132→
   133→## Working with Claude Code
   134→
   135→The team uses Claude Code as the primary interface for managing work. Claude enforces standards and automates governance.
   136→
   137→### What Claude Does Automatically
   138→
   139→| Action | When | What |
   140→|--------|------|------|
   141→| Apply tag standards | Work item creation | Enforces tag taxonomy rules |
   142→| Set area path | Work item creation | Maps to correct team based on content |
   143→| Daily validation | Scheduled or `/validate-work` | Audits states, area paths, tags, grooming |
   144→| Checkpoint | ~50 turns or topic shift | Saves session state, updates work items |
   145→| Sync to DevOps | Session end | Creates/updates work items from conversation |
   146→
   147→### Key Commands
   148→
   149→| Say This | Claude Does |
   150→|----------|-------------|
   151→| "start work" | Loads session context, checks MCPs |
   152→| "end work" / "done for now" | Full checkpoint, syncs work items |
   153→| "groom the backlog" | Reviews and refines Proposed items |
   154→| "what's the status" | Shows current work item states |
   155→| "validate" | Runs full work item audit |
   156→| "brain dump" | Captures unstructured ideas into backlog |
   157→
   158→### Daily Validation Checks
   159→
   160→Claude audits work items for:
   161→
   162→- [ ] State correctness (no Active items without assignment)
   163→- [ ] Area path matches team ownership
   164→- [ ] Minimum tags present (domain + work-type)
   165→- [ ] Groomed items have descriptions and acceptance criteria
   166→- [ ] No orphan items at root area path
   167→- [ ] Archived items are in Archive area path
   168→
   169→## Iterations
   170→
   171→| Iteration | Dates | Purpose |
   172→|-----------|-------|---------|
   173→| Backlog | 2026 full year | Default for Kanban flow items |
   174→| Q1 2026 | Jan 1 - Mar 31 | Planned Q1 project work |
   175→| Q2 2026 | Apr 1 - Jun 30 | Planned Q2 project work |
   176→
   177→All 5 teams have access to all iterations.
   178→
   179→## Area Path Detail
   180→
   181→### Software Engineering
   182→```
   183→Information Technology
   184→  Software Engineering
   185→    API Management
   186→    Architecture
   187→    Custom Application Development
   188→    DevOps and CI-CD
   189→    Enterprise Application Development
   190→    Integration
   191→    Testing and QA
   192→```
   193→
   194→### Data Services
   195→```
   196→Information Technology
   197→  Data Services
   198→    Data Analytics
   199→    Data Architecture
   200→    Data Governance
   201→    Data Integration
   202→    Data Quality
   203→    Report Development
   204→```
   205→
   206→### Infrastructure Management
   207→```
   208→Information Technology
   209→  Infrastructure Management
   210→    Asset Management
   211→    Backup and Disaster Recovery
   212→    Capacity Planning
   213→    Cloud Infrastructure
   214→    Database Management
   215→    Endpoint Management
   216→    Hardware Management
   217→    Network Management
   218→    Server and Storage Management
   219→    Software Management
   220→```
   221→
   222→### Cybersecurity
   223→```
   224→Information Technology
   225→  Cybersecurity
   226→    Compliance and Regulatory
   227→    Identity and Access Management
   228→    Incident Response
   229→    Literacy Training and Awareness
   230→    Risk Assessment
   231→    Security Operations
   232→    Vulnerability Management
   233→```
   234→
   235→### Help Desk and Support
   236→```
   237→Information Technology
   238→  Help Desk and Support
   239→    Change Management
   240→    Configuration Management
   241→    Helpdesk Ticketing
   242→    Incident Management
   243→    Knowledge Management
   244→    Problem Management
   245→    Release Management
   246→    Service Request Management
   247→    User Training and Documentation
   248→```
   249→
   250→## Naming Conventions
   251→
   252→| System | Pattern | Example |
   253→|--------|---------|---------|
   254→| Work Item Title | `[Action]: [Description]` | `Implement: Salesforce SSO JIT provisioning` |
   255→| GitHub Branch | `[type]/[work-item-id]-[slug]` | `feature/1101-salesforce-sso` |
   256→| Git Commit | `[type]: [description] (#[work-item])` | `feat: add SSO config (#1101)` |
   257→
   258→## Getting Started (New Team Member)
   259→
   260→See the [Onboarding Guide](./onboarding-guide.md) for a complete walkthrough. Quick start:
   261→
   262→1. **Get ADO access**: Request access to SafariCircuitsLLC organization
   263→2. **Install Claude Code**: Follow setup guide in `shared/claude-workflow/`
   264→3. **Browse your board**: Navigate to your team's board view in ADO
   265→4. **Start your first task**: Tell Claude "start work" and pick a Groomed item
   266→5. **Follow the flow**: Proposed (backlog) > Active (working) > Resolved (done, needs validation) > Closed
   267→6. **Tag everything**: Claude will remind you, but always include domain + work-type tags
   268→7. **End your session**: Tell Claude "done for now" to checkpoint and sync
   269→
   270→## Related Documentation
   271→
   272→### Onboarding & Work Instructions
   273→
   274→- [Onboarding Guide](./onboarding-guide.md) - Start here as a new team member
   275→- [Working a Task](./work-instructions/working-a-task.md) - Daily workflow with Claude
   276→- [Creating Work Items](./work-instructions/creating-work-items.md) - Hierarchy, classification, and tags
   277→- [Grooming the Backlog](./work-instructions/grooming-backlog.md) - Refining items for readiness
   278→
   279→### Reference
   280→
   281→- [DevOps Workflow Reference](~/.claude/reference/devops-workflow.md) - Naming conventions, sync points
   282→- [Tag Taxonomy](~/.claude/reference/devops-workflow.md#tag-taxonomy) - Full tag reference

     2→

     5→title: Azure DevOps API Integration Guide

    11→
    12→## Overview
    13→
    14→This document specifies the Azure DevOps REST API integration for Safari Analyze. It covers authentication, API endpoints, rate limiting, and error handling strategies.
    15→
    16→## Connection Details
    17→
    18→| Setting | Value |
    19→|---------|-------|
    20→| Organization | SafariCircuitsLLC |
    21→| Organization URL | https://dev.azure.com/SafariCircuitsLLC |
    22→| Project | Information Technology |
    23→| Project ID | 18ba500c-2736-41cc-b6d7-695a3751dc96 |
    24→| API Version | 7.1 |
    25→| Base URL | https://dev.azure.com/SafariCircuitsLLC/Information%20Technology/_apis |
    26→
    27→---
    28→
    29→## Authentication
    30→
    31→### Option 1: Personal Access Token (PAT)
    32→
    33→**Use for:** Development, testing, small-scale integrations
    34→
    35→| Scope | Permission | Required For |
    36→|-------|------------|--------------|
    37→| Work Items | Read | Work item queries |
    38→| Code | Read | Commits, PRs, repositories |
    39→| Build | Read | Build definitions, builds |
    40→| Project and Team | Read | Teams, iterations, capacity |
    41→
    42→**Header Format:**
    43→```
    44→Authorization: Basic {base64(:{PAT})}
    45→```
    46→
    47→**Example:**
    48→```python
    49→import base64
    50→
    51→pat = "your-personal-access-token"
    52→auth_header = base64.b64encode(f":{pat}".encode()).decode()
    53→headers = {
    54→    "Authorization": f"Basic {auth_header}",
    55→    "Content-Type": "application/json"
    56→}
    57→```
    58→
    59→### Option 2: Service Principal (OAuth)
    60→
    61→**Use for:** Production, automated pipelines, service accounts
    62→
    63→**Setup Steps:**
    64→1. Register app in Azure AD
    65→2. Grant API permissions for Azure DevOps
    66→3. Add service principal to ADO organization
    67→4. Grant appropriate access to project

    75→    client_id="your-client-id",
    76→    client_secret="your-client-secret"
    77→)
    78→
    79→token = credential.get_token("499b84ac-1321-427f-aa17-267ca6975798/.default")

    81→    "Authorization": f"Bearer {token.token}",

    86→### Recommended: Managed Identity (Azure-hosted)

   213→## Rate Limiting
   214→
   215→### Azure DevOps Rate Limits
   216→
   217→| Limit Type | Value | Scope |
   218→|------------|-------|-------|
   219→| Requests per second | 200 | Per user/PAT |
   220→| Concurrent requests | 25 | Per user/PAT |
   221→| Response size | 16 MB | Per request |
   222→| WIQL results | 20,000 | Per query |
   223→| Batch work items | 200 | Per request |
   224→
   225→### Rate Limit Headers
   226→
   227→```
   228→X-RateLimit-Limit: 200
   229→X-RateLimit-Remaining: 195
   230→X-RateLimit-Reset: 2026-01-20T15:01:00Z
   231→Retry-After: 5
   232→```
   233→
   234→### Client-Side Rate Limiting
   235→
   236→```python
   237→import asyncio
   238→from aiolimiter import AsyncLimiter
   239→
   240→class ADOClient:
   241→    def __init__(self):
   242→        # 150 requests per second (conservative margin)
   243→        self.rate_limiter = AsyncLimiter(150, 1)
   244→        # Max 20 concurrent requests
   245→        self.semaphore = asyncio.Semaphore(20)
   246→
   247→    async def request(self, method: str, url: str, **kwargs):
   248→        async with self.rate_limiter:
   249→            async with self.semaphore:
   250→                async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:
   251→                    async with session.request(method, url, **kwargs) as response:
   252→                        if response.status == 429:
   253→                            retry_after = int(response.headers.get('Retry-After', 5))
   254→                            await asyncio.sleep(retry_after)
   255→                            return await self.request(method, url, **kwargs)
   256→                        return await response.json()
   257→```

   260→
   261→## Error Handling
   262→
   263→### HTTP Status Codes
   264→
   265→| Status | Meaning | Action |
   266→|--------|---------|--------|
   267→| 200 | Success | Process response |
   268→| 201 | Created | Process response |
   269→| 400 | Bad Request | Log error, fix request |
   270→| 401 | Unauthorized | Refresh token, retry |
   271→| 403 | Forbidden | Check permissions, alert |
   272→| 404 | Not Found | Log, skip item |
   273→| 429 | Rate Limited | Backoff, retry |
   274→| 500 | Server Error | Exponential backoff, retry |
   275→| 503 | Service Unavailable | Exponential backoff, retry |
   276→
   277→### Retry Strategy
   278→
   279→```python
   280→from tenacity import (
   281→    retry,
   282→    stop_after_attempt,
   283→    wait_exponential,
   284→    retry_if_exception_type
   285→)
   286→
   287→class RetryableError(Exception):
   288→    pass
   289→
   290→class ADOClient:
   291→    @retry(
   292→        stop=stop_after_attempt(5),
   293→        wait=wait_exponential(multiplier=1, min=1, max=60),
   294→        retry=retry_if_exception_type(RetryableError)
   295→    )
   296→    async def request_with_retry(self, method: str, url: str, **kwargs):
   297→        response = await self.session.request(method, url, **kwargs)
   298→
   299→        if response.status in [429, 500, 502, 503, 504]:
   300→            raise RetryableError(f"Retryable error: {response.status}")
   301→
   302→        if response.status == 401:
   303→            await self.refresh_token()
   304→            raise RetryableError("Token refreshed, retrying")
   305→
   306→        response.raise_for_status()
   307→        return await response.json()
   308→```
   309→
   310→### Error Response Handling
   311→
   312→```python
   313→async def handle_response(response):
   314→    if response.status >= 400:
   315→        error_body = await response.json()
   316→
   317→        error_info = {
   318→            'status': response.status,
   319→            'message': error_body.get('message', 'Unknown error'),
   320→            'typeKey': error_body.get('typeKey'),
   321→            'errorCode': error_body.get('errorCode'),
   322→            'customProperties': error_body.get('customProperties', {})
   323→        }
   324→
   325→        logger.error(f"ADO API Error: {error_info}")
   326→
   327→        if response.status == 404:
   328→            return None  # Item not found, return None
   329→        elif response.status == 401:
   330→            raise AuthenticationError(error_info)
   331→        elif response.status == 403:
   332→            raise PermissionError(error_info)
   333→        elif response.status == 429:
   334→            raise RateLimitError(error_info)
   335→        else:
   336→            raise ADOAPIError(error_info)
   337→
   338→    return await response.json()
   339→```

   342→
   343→## Pagination
   344→
   345→### Continuation Token Pattern
   346→
   347→Many ADO APIs use continuation tokens for pagination:
   348→
   349→```python
   350→async def paginate_all(client, url, params=None):
   351→    """Fetch all pages using continuation token."""
   352→    params = params or {}
   353→    all_items = []
   354→
   355→    while True:
   356→        response = await client.request('GET', url, params=params)
   357→
   358→        items = response.get('value', [])
   359→        all_items.extend(items)
   360→
   361→        continuation_token = response.get('continuationToken')
   362→        if not continuation_token:
   363→            break
   364→
   365→        params['continuationToken'] = continuation_token
   366→
   367→    return all_items
   368→```
   369→
   370→### Top/Skip Pattern
   371→
   372→For APIs using top/skip:
   373→
   374→```python
   375→async def paginate_with_skip(client, url, page_size=100):
   376→    """Fetch all pages using $top and $skip."""
   377→    all_items = []
   378→    skip = 0
   379→
   380→    while True:
   381→        params = {'$top': page_size, '$skip': skip}
   382→        response = await client.request('GET', url, params=params)
   383→
   384→        items = response.get('value', [])
   385→        if not items:
   386→            break
   387→
   388→        all_items.extend(items)
   389→        skip += page_size
   390→
   391→        # Stop if we got fewer items than page size
   392→        if len(items) < page_size:
   393→            break
   394→
   395→    return all_items
   396→```

   578→### Credential Management
   579→
   580→| Environment | Recommendation |

   582→| Local Dev | Environment variables, `.env` file |

   584→| Production | Managed Identity or Key Vault |

   586→### Secret Rotation

   103→```sql
   104→SELECT
   105→    velocity AS current_value,
   106→    LAG(velocity) OVER (ORDER BY finish_date) AS previous_value,
   107→    ROUND((velocity - LAG(velocity) OVER (ORDER BY finish_date))
   108→          / NULLIF(LAG(velocity) OVER (ORDER BY finish_date), 0) * 100, 1) AS trend_pct
   109→FROM devops.v_team_velocity
   110→WHERE team_id = :team_id
   111→ORDER BY finish_date DESC
   112→LIMIT 1;
   113→```
   114→

   122→### 2. Sprint Burndown Chart
   123→
   124→| Attribute | Value |
   125→|-----------|-------|
   126→| Type | `line` |
   127→| Data Source | `devops.work_item`, `devops.sprint` |
   128→| Refresh | Every 5 minutes |
   129→
   130→```sql
   131→WITH daily_remaining AS (
   132→    SELECT
   133→        d.date,
   134→        SUM(CASE
   135→            WHEN wih.changed_date <= d.date THEN
   136→                COALESCE((wih.new_value)::decimal, 0)
   137→            ELSE wi.remaining_work
   138→        END) AS remaining
   139→    FROM generate_series(
   140→        (SELECT start_date FROM devops.sprint WHERE id = :sprint_id),
   141→        (SELECT finish_date FROM devops.sprint WHERE id = :sprint_id),
   142→        '1 day'::interval
   143→    ) d(date)
   144→    CROSS JOIN devops.work_item wi
   145→    LEFT JOIN devops.work_item_history wih
   146→        ON wi.id = wih.work_item_id
   147→        AND wih.field = 'Microsoft.VSTS.Scheduling.RemainingWork'
   148→    WHERE wi.sprint_id = :sprint_id
   149→    GROUP BY d.date
   150→),
   151→ideal_burn AS (
   152→    SELECT
   153→        d.date,
   154→        total_work - (total_work * (ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY d.date) - 1)
   155→            / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM generate_series(start_date, finish_date, '1 day'))) AS ideal
   156→    FROM generate_series(start_date, finish_date, '1 day') d(date),
   157→         (SELECT start_date, finish_date, SUM(remaining_work) as total_work
   158→          FROM devops.sprint s
   159→          JOIN devops.work_item wi ON wi.sprint_id = s.id
   160→          WHERE s.id = :sprint_id
   161→          GROUP BY start_date, finish_date) s
   162→)
   163→SELECT
   164→    dr.date,
   165→    dr.remaining AS actual,
   166→    ib.ideal
   167→FROM daily_remaining dr
   168→JOIN ideal_burn ib ON dr.date = ib.date
   169→ORDER BY dr.date;
   170→```
   171→

   175→| Actual Line | Solid, Safari Red (#9B1C1C) |
   176→| Trend Line | Dotted, blue (#60A5FA) |
   177→| Grid | Subtle, #334155 |
   178→| Background | #1E293B |

   238→### 5. Team Capacity vs Allocation
   239→
   240→| Attribute | Value |
   241→|-----------|-------|
   242→| Type | `grouped-bar` |
   243→| Data Source | `devops.capacity`, `devops.work_item` |
   244→| Refresh | Daily |
   245→
   246→```sql
   247→SELECT
   248→    tm.display_name,
   249→    c.hours_per_day * (s.finish_date - s.start_date + 1) AS capacity_hours,
   250→    COALESCE(SUM(wi.remaining_work), 0) AS allocated_hours,
   251→    ROUND(COALESCE(SUM(wi.remaining_work), 0) * 100.0
   252→          / NULLIF(c.hours_per_day * (s.finish_date - s.start_date + 1), 0), 1) AS utilization_pct
   253→FROM devops.team_member tm
   254→JOIN devops.capacity c ON c.member_id = tm.id
   255→JOIN devops.sprint s ON s.id = c.sprint_id
   256→LEFT JOIN devops.work_item wi ON wi.assigned_to_id = tm.id AND wi.sprint_id = s.id
   257→WHERE s.id = :sprint_id
   258→GROUP BY tm.display_name, c.hours_per_day, s.start_date, s.finish_date
   259→ORDER BY tm.display_name;
   260→```

    68→### Batch ETL Architecture
    69→
    70→```python
    71→# batch_etl/work_items.py
    72→
    73→async def extract_work_items(since: datetime = None) -> list[dict]:
    74→    """Extract work items from ADO API."""
    75→    wiql = """
    76→    SELECT [System.Id], [System.Rev], [System.ChangedDate]
    77→    FROM WorkItems
    78→    WHERE [System.TeamProject] = 'Information Technology'
    79→    """
    80→    if since:
    81→        wiql += f" AND [System.ChangedDate] > '{since.isoformat()}'"
    82→
    83→    result = await ado_client.query_wiql(wiql)
    84→    work_item_ids = [item['id'] for item in result['workItems']]
    85→
    86→    # Batch fetch full work items (API limit: 200 per request)
    87→    work_items = []
    88→    for batch in chunks(work_item_ids, 200):
    89→        items = await ado_client.get_work_items_batch(batch, expand='all')
    90→        work_items.extend(items)
    91→
    92→    return work_items
    93→
    94→
    95→def transform_work_item(ado_item: dict) -> dict:
    96→    """Transform ADO work item to database model."""
    97→    fields = ado_item['fields']
    98→    return {
    99→        'id': ado_item['id'],
   100→        'type': fields['System.WorkItemType'],
   101→        'title': fields['System.Title'],
   102→        'state': fields['System.State'],
   103→        'reason': fields.get('System.Reason'),
   104→        'assigned_to_name': fields.get('System.AssignedTo', {}).get('displayName'),
   105→        'area_path': fields.get('System.AreaPath'),
   106→        'iteration_path': fields.get('System.IterationPath'),
   107→        'parent_id': extract_parent_id(ado_item.get('relations', [])),
   108→        'priority': fields.get('Microsoft.VSTS.Common.Priority'),
   109→        'effort': fields.get('Microsoft.VSTS.Scheduling.Effort'),
   110→        'story_points': fields.get('Microsoft.VSTS.Scheduling.Size'),
   111→        'remaining_work': fields.get('Microsoft.VSTS.Scheduling.RemainingWork'),
   112→        'created_by': fields['System.CreatedBy']['displayName'],
   113→        'created_date': parse_datetime(fields['System.CreatedDate']),
   114→        'changed_by': fields.get('System.ChangedBy', {}).get('displayName'),
   115→        'changed_date': parse_datetime(fields.get('System.ChangedDate')),
   116→        'revision': ado_item['rev'],
   117→        'url': ado_item['url']
   118→    }
   119→
   120→
   121→async def load_work_items(work_items: list[dict]):
   122→    """Upsert work items to PostgreSQL."""
   123→    async with db.transaction():
   124→        for item in work_items:
   125→            await db.execute("""
   126→                INSERT INTO devops.work_item (id, type, title, state, ...)
   127→                VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, ...)
   128→                ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE SET
   129→                    type = EXCLUDED.type,
   130→                    title = EXCLUDED.title,
   131→                    state = EXCLUDED.state,
   132→                    ...
   133→                    updated_at = NOW()
   134→            """, item.values())
   135→```

   170→
   171→### CDC Producer Implementation
   172→
   173→```python
   174→# streaming/cdc_producer.py
   175→
   176→class WorkItemCDCProducer:
   177→    def __init__(self, kafka_producer, redis_client, ado_client):
   178→        self.producer = kafka_producer
   179→        self.redis = redis_client
   180→        self.ado = ado_client
   181→        self.poll_interval = 60  # seconds
   182→
   183→    async def run(self):
   184→        """Main polling loop."""
   185→        while True:
   186→            try:
   187→                await self.detect_and_emit_changes()
   188→                await asyncio.sleep(self.poll_interval)
   189→            except Exception as e:
   190→                logger.error(f"CDC poll failed: {e}")
   191→                await asyncio.sleep(self.poll_interval * 2)  # backoff
   192→
   193→    async def detect_and_emit_changes(self):
   194→        """Detect changes since last poll and emit events."""
   195→        last_poll = await self.redis.get('devops:cdc:last_poll')
   196→        last_poll_dt = datetime.fromisoformat(last_poll) if last_poll else datetime.min
   197→
   198→        # Query for changed work items
   199→        changed_items = await self.ado.query_wiql(f"""
   200→            SELECT [System.Id], [System.Rev]
   201→            FROM WorkItems
   202→            WHERE [System.ChangedDate] > '{last_poll_dt.isoformat()}'
   203→            ORDER BY [System.ChangedDate] ASC
   204→        """)
   205→
   206→        for item in changed_items:
   207→            await self.process_work_item_change(item['id'], item['rev'])
   208→
   209→        # Update last poll time
   210→        await self.redis.set('devops:cdc:last_poll', datetime.utcnow().isoformat())
   211→
   212→    async def process_work_item_change(self, work_item_id: int, current_rev: int):
   213→        """Process a single work item change."""
   214→        stored_rev = await self.redis.get(f'devops:wi:{work_item_id}:rev')
   215→        stored_rev = int(stored_rev) if stored_rev else 0
   216→
   217→        if current_rev <= stored_rev:
   218→            return  # No change
   219→
   220→        # Fetch full work item with revisions
   221→        work_item = await self.ado.get_work_item(work_item_id, expand='all')
   222→
   223→        # Determine event type
   224→        if stored_rev == 0:
   225→            event_type = 'created'
   226→        else:
   227→            event_type = 'updated'
   228→
   229→        # Build event
   230→        event = {
   231→            'eventId': str(uuid.uuid4()),
   232→            'eventType': event_type,
   233→            'eventTime': datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
   234→            'source': 'ado.safaricircuitsllc.information-technology',
   235→            'data': {
   236→                'workItemId': work_item_id,
   237→                'workItemType': work_item['fields']['System.WorkItemType'],
   238→                'revision': current_rev,
   239→                'fields': work_item['fields'],
   240→                'changedFields': self.diff_revisions(stored_rev, current_rev, work_item),
   241→                'changedBy': work_item['fields'].get('System.ChangedBy', {}).get('displayName'),
   242→                'changedDate': work_item['fields'].get('System.ChangedDate')
   243→            }
   244→        }
   245→
   246→        # Emit to Kafka
   247→        topic = f'safari.devops.workitem.{event_type}'
   248→        await self.producer.send(topic, key=str(work_item_id), value=event)
   249→
   250→        # Update stored revision
   251→        await self.redis.set(f'devops:wi:{work_item_id}:rev', current_rev)
   252→```

   498→WITH ado_counts AS (
